meta.siteDescription: The digital maturity ranking of French car dealer groups. 16 groups audited, 87 criteria, 4 dimensions.

hero.title: Digital Auto Rankings France 2026
hero.subtitle: The digital maturity ranking of French car dealer groups
hero.description: 16 groups audited. 87 criteria. 4 dimensions. Full transparency.

editorial.title: What is digital maturity for a car dealer group?
editorial.intro: The car buying journey starts online. 87% of car buyers research online before visiting a dealership. The quality of a dealer group's digital infrastructure is no longer a competitive advantage — it's a prerequisite for relevance.
editorial.whatDescription: The Digital Auto Rankings evaluates each group across 4 complementary dimensions, for a total score out of 400 points:
dimensions: Technical SEO — Can Google properly crawl and index the site?
SEO Content — Is the content deep, authoritative, and optimized for search?
Email & Deliverability — Do the domain's emails reach the inbox?
AI Citation — Can ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google AI Overviews cite this site?
editorial.transparencyDescription: Every score is based on 87 documented criteria, applied identically to all groups. The data used is exclusively public: DNS, HTML, robots.txt, sitemaps, HTTP headers. No private access, no favoritism. The full methodology is published on this site.
editorial.producedBy: This ranking is produced by ElPi Corp / Perello Consulting, a consulting firm specializing in AI-assisted digital transformation. The audit is conducted by a team of specialized AI agents, orchestrated by Laurent Perello.

ranking.title: Full ranking
ranking.description: All 16 French car dealer groups, ranked by digital maturity.

editorial.howToReadDescription: Each group is evaluated across 4 dimensions (Technical SEO, SEO Content, Email & Deliverability, AI Citation), each scored out of 100 points. The total score (out of 400) determines the ranking. Click on a group to access its detailed audit with findings and recommendations.
editorial.tiersExplained: The tiers reflect overall digital maturity: Tier A (260+) designates leaders, Tier B (210-259) developing groups, Tier C (160-209) lagging ones, and Tier D (0-159) critical situations requiring immediate remediation.
editorial.methodologyLink: See our full methodology to understand the 87 criteria used.

group.downloadDescription: Receive all 16 audit deliverables for this group by email.

dimensionContext.seoTechnical: Technical SEO evaluates whether Google can properly crawl and index the site. Issues here directly impact the group's organic visibility.
dimensionContext.seoContent: SEO Content measures the depth, authority, and structure of content. A low score means content too thin to compete on key queries.
dimensionContext.email: Email & Deliverability checks whether the domain is properly configured so emails reach the inbox (SPF, DKIM, DMARC).
dimensionContext.aiCitation: AI Citation measures whether AI engines (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Google AI) can cite this site. This is the weakest dimension industry-wide, averaging 28/100.

methodology.title: Methodology
methodology.description: How we measure digital maturity. 87 criteria, 4 dimensions, full transparency.

overview.title: Overview
overview.description: Each group is evaluated across 4 dimensions, each scored out of 100 points, for a maximum total of 400 points.

seoTechnical.title: D1 — Technical SEO
seoTechnical.description: Can Google crawl and index the site properly? Crawlability, indexability, security, URL structure, mobile, Core Web Vitals.

seoContent.title: D2 — SEO Content
seoContent.description: Is the content deep, authoritative, and well-structured? E-E-A-T signals, depth, readability, blog, internal linking.

email.title: D3 — Email & Deliverability
email.description: Can the domain send emails that reach the inbox? SPF, DKIM, DMARC, blacklists, TLS.

aiCitation.title: D4 — AI Citation
aiCitation.description: Can ChatGPT, Perplexity, Google AI cite this site? Schema.org, FAQ, entity clarity, AI bot access.

A.label: Tier A
A.description: Digital leaders. Strong foundations, proactive AI readiness.

B.label: Tier B
B.description: Developing. Basics covered but significant gaps.

C.label: Tier C
C.description: Lagging. Multiple issues. Digital presence is a liability.

D.label: Tier D
D.description: Critical. Fundamental failures. Immediate remediation required.

tools.title: Tools and methods
tools.description: All scores are derived from specialized automated agents. Pipeline: Firecrawl scraping, DNS analysis, SEO agents, email agents, AI agents.

transparency.title: Transparency
items: Scores based exclusively on publicly accessible data
Email audit based on DNS infrastructure, not deliverability metrics
Performance scores from a single geographic location (Paris)
Content assessment is point-in-time (audit date stated)
AI citation readiness is an emerging field — criteria will evolve

items.question: How are scores calculated?
items.answer: Each dimension is scored out of 100 points from specific automated checks. The total score is the sum of all 4 dimensions (max 400). Tiers are assigned based on fixed thresholds.

items.question: Is the data public?
items.answer: Yes. All scores are derived from publicly accessible data: DNS, HTML, robots.txt, sitemaps, HTTP headers. No private access (Google Search Console, analytics) is used.

items.question: How often is the ranking updated?
items.answer: The ranking is updated quarterly. Each update includes a full re-scan of all groups.

items.question: How to challenge a score?
items.answer: Each audited group can request the full audit report and challenge findings. If a fix has been deployed since the audit, we re-scan and adjust the score.

items.question: Why is AI Citation a dimension?
items.answer: AI engines (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Google AI Overviews) increasingly answer automotive queries. Groups with structured data get cited. Others are invisible.

insights.title: Industry insights
insights.description: Trends, blind spots, and best practices discovered across 16 groups.

editorial.intro: The analysis of 16 French car dealer groups reveals a sector deeply misaligned between its digital ambitions and the reality of its infrastructure. While investments in physical dealerships remain massive, the digital infrastructure — the one that greets 87% of buyers on first contact — is widely under-invested.
editorial.seoTechTitle: Technical SEO: the foundation is fragile
editorial.seoTechBody: The industry average for Technical SEO stands at 54/100. The problems are structural: missing or duplicate canonicals, incomplete sitemaps, absent security headers. 8 of 16 groups have zero HTTP security headers. Several still run Apache versions dating from 2017. The leaders (Aramis Auto, Autosphere) stand out with clean technical infrastructure: forced HTTPS, complete sitemaps, and controlled load times.
editorial.seoContentTitle: SEO Content: blogs empty of substance
editorial.seoContentBody: Average: 48/100. Most groups have a news or blog section, but content is systematically shallow: 100-400 word articles, no keyword strategy, no internal linking. E-E-A-T signals (Experience, Expertise, Authoritativeness, Trustworthiness) are virtually nonexistent — no author pages, no citations, no in-depth content. Top performers differentiate with long-form content (1,500+ words) and coherent heading structures.
editorial.emailTitle: Email & Deliverability: the strongest dimension, but a false sense of security
editorial.emailBody: At 62/100, email is the sector's strongest dimension. Most groups use Google Workspace or Microsoft 365 with SPF and DKIM configured. But the detail reveals a massive issue: 9 of 16 groups have a DMARC policy set to p=none, meaning monitoring only. In practice, anyone can send emails spoofing their domain. For groups handling transactions worth tens of thousands of euros, this is an unaddressed critical risk.
editorial.aiCitationTitle: AI Citation: the most dangerous blind spot (28/100)
editorial.aiCitationBody: This is the most alarming finding of this edition. At 28/100 average, AI citation readiness is catastrophic. What does this score mean? AI engines like ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google AI Overviews now directly answer consumer questions: "What's the best dealership near me?", "Reliability of brand X", "Service cost at group Y". Sites with structured data (Schema.org), FAQs, and strong semantic clarity get cited. The rest are invisible. 11 of 16 groups have zero or near-zero JSON-LD markup. None implement LocalBusiness or AutoDealer schema at the scale of their dealership networks. The best score (71/100, Aramis Auto) is the only one above the 50 mark.
editorial.bestPracticesBody: The top-ranking groups (Aramis Auto, Autosphere) share common traits: clean technical infrastructure (no 404s, complete sitemaps, forced HTTPS), structured content with Schema.org data, and a proactive approach to AI visibility. This isn't a budget question — several smaller groups outperform billion-euro revenue groups because they invested in foundations rather than cosmetics.
commonFailuresItems: DMARC at p=none: 56% of groups vulnerable to email spoofing
Zero structured data: 69% of groups without JSON-LD schema
Shallow content: 100-400 word articles with no SEO strategy
Missing security headers: 50% of groups unprotected
Obsolete servers: Apache 2.4.29 (2017) still in production
editorial.comparisonBody: For context: in French e-commerce, average digital maturity scores exceed 280/400. The banking sector sits around 300/400. The automotive industry, at 207/400 average, lags significantly. The sector invests in digital sales transformation (online configurators, financing journeys), but neglects the foundations: technical SEO, authoritative content, and presence in AI-generated answers.

blindSpot.title: The industry blind spot: AI Citation
blindSpot.description: At 28/100 average, AI citation readiness is the biggest structural weakness of the French automotive sector.

items.title: Email is the strongest dimension
items.description: Average 62/100. Most groups use Google Workspace or Microsoft 365 with basic SPF/DKIM.

items.title: DMARC p=none is epidemic
items.description: 9 of 16 groups have DMARC at p=none (monitoring only). Anyone can spoof their email domains.

items.title: Zero structured data is the norm
items.description: 11 of 16 groups have zero or near-zero JSON-LD Schema.org markup.

items.title: Blogs exist but are content-light
items.description: Most have a blog or news section but content is shallow (100-400 words), with no SEO strategy.

items.title: No LocalBusiness schema deployed
items.description: 1,500+ combined dealerships across 16 groups, not a single one implements LocalBusiness or AutoDealer at scale.

items.title: Security headers widely neglected
items.description: 8 of 16 groups have zero HTTP security headers. Multiple groups still run Apache 2.4.29 (2017).

about.title: About
about.description: Who we are and why we built this ranking.

who.title: Who we are
who.description: The Digital Auto Rankings France is produced by ElPi Corp / Perello Consulting, a consulting firm specializing in AI-driven digital transformation. Based in Strasbourg, the firm helps businesses of all sizes optimize their online presence, structure their data, and improve their visibility across both traditional search engines and emerging AI engines.

founder.title: Laurent Perello
founder.bio: 25+ years of experience in tech, from Web 1.0 to AI architectures. Founder of ElPi Corp / Perello Consulting. Based in Strasbourg, France.
founder.expertise: Specialist in AI-assisted digital transformation for businesses. Laurent works with executives, consultants, and technical teams to adopt AI tools that optimize their digital presence, productivity, and competitiveness. His background spans web development, distributed systems architecture, strategic consulting, and now applied AI engineering for business.
founder.approach: His approach: build systems that work, not slide decks. Every audit, every recommendation is backed by verifiable data and specialized AI agents that produce concrete deliverables. No theory disconnected from practice. No negotiated scores. Facts, numbers, actions.

why.title: Why this ranking
why.description: The French automotive industry invests massively in physical dealerships but systematically neglects its digital infrastructure. This ranking makes that reality measurable.
why.extended: We built this ranking because no independent, transparent benchmark existed for the digital maturity of French car dealer groups. We practice what we preach: every score is based on 87 documented criteria, and our methodology is fully public. No black box, no favoritism, no negotiated scores. Transparency is not a marketing argument — it's the condition of credibility.

team.title: The team
team.description: This ranking is produced by a team of specialized AI agents, orchestrated by Laurent Perello. Each dimension is audited by a dedicated agent trained on industry best practices. The Technical SEO agent verifies 27 control points. The SEO Content agent evaluates depth, authority, and E-E-A-T signals. The Email agent analyzes the complete DNS infrastructure (SPF, DKIM, DMARC, blacklists). The AI Citation agent evaluates readiness for generative engines (Schema.org, FAQ, semantic clarity). Together, they produce a reproducible, objective, and exhaustive audit — the same pipeline applied to every group, without exception.

items.question: Who produces this ranking?
items.answer: The Digital Auto Rankings France is produced by ElPi Corp / Perello Consulting, a consulting firm specializing in AI-assisted digital transformation. The audit is conducted by a team of specialized AI agents, orchestrated by Laurent Perello.

items.question: How are scores calculated?
items.answer: Each group is evaluated across 4 dimensions (Technical SEO, SEO Content, Email & Deliverability, AI Citation), each scored out of 100 points from automated checks. The total score (max 400) determines the ranking. All criteria are documented on our Methodology page.

items.question: How often is the ranking updated?
items.answer: The ranking is updated quarterly. Each update includes a full re-scan of all groups using the same methodology.

items.question: Can my group be audited?
items.answer: Yes. Any French car dealer group can request a free audit through our form. You will receive your complete score within 48 hours.

items.question: Is this ranking independent?
items.answer: Yes. No group pays to appear in the ranking, and no score is negotiated. The data used is exclusively public (DNS, HTML, robots.txt, sitemaps, HTTP headers). Our methodology is fully published.

items.question: How can I request a correction?
items.answer: Each audited group has a right of rectification. If you find an error or a fix has been deployed since the audit, contact us at contact@perello.consulting. We will re-scan and adjust the score.

Audited groups have a right of rectification. Any contested and corrected finding results in a re-scan and score adjustment.

liability.description: Information provided on this site is for informational purposes. ElPi Corp / Perello Consulting strives to ensure data accuracy but cannot guarantee the absence of errors. Use of the information is at the user's sole responsibility.

dataCollected.title: Data collected
dataCollected.description: We only collect data you voluntarily provide through the audit request form:

purpose.title: Purpose of processing
purpose.description: Your data is used exclusively to process your digital audit request and contact you with the results. It is never sold, rented, or shared with third parties.

legalBasis.title: Legal basis
legalBasis.description: Processing is based on your consent, expressed by submitting the form and checking the consent checkbox.

retention.title: Retention period
retention.description: Your data is retained for 12 months from your request. After that period, it is deleted unless a business relationship has been established.

rights.title: Your rights
rights.description: Under the GDPR, you have the following rights:
items: Right of access: obtain a copy of your personal data
Right of rectification: correct inaccurate data
Right to erasure: request deletion of your data
Right to portability: receive your data in a structured format
Right to object: oppose the processing of your data
rights.contact: To exercise these rights, contact us: contact@perello.consulting.

cookies.title: Cookies and tracking
cookies.description: This site does not use any advertising cookies or analytics tracking tools. No tracking pixels are deployed. Only essential technical cookies required for site operation may be used.

security.title: Security
security.description: We implement the necessary technical and organizational measures to protect your data against unauthorized access, loss, or alteration.
